title: A cell atlas foundation model for scalable search of similar human cells
data_sources: GEO GSE280632 (new in vitro data generated in this study)
core_conclusion: SCimilarity is a deep metric-learning foundation model that encodes 23.4 million single-cell RNA-seq profiles from 412 studies into a unified 128-dimensional hyperspherical space, enabling similarity searches in 0.05 seconds. Applied to interstitial lung disease macrophages, it retrieved fibrosis-associated cell states across multiple fibrotic diseases and identified a 3D hydrogel PBMC culture as the top in vitro recapitulation of the disease state — experimentally validated. It outperforms scGPT and scFoundation on cell-state retrieval while matching dedicated batch-integration methods.
why_it_matters: Scales cell similarity search from minutes/hours to sub-second; provides annotation-free, cross-study, cross-tissue, cross-disease cell comparison; bridges in vivo and in vitro biology computationally with experimental validation; delivers interpretable gene attributions via distance-based Integrated Gradients; released as fully open model weights, indices, and code.
why_nature: Pan-body foundation model validated across 30 tissues and 132 diseases; includes experimental wet-lab validation closing the computational loop; advances the Human Cell Atlas program's scalability; demonstrates concrete superiority over existing single-cell foundation models on retrieval tasks.
added_value_to_my_research: Contrastive triplet loss over Place Ontology -> universal place-similarity encoder for neighborhood retrieval across cities (geospatial foundation model fine-tuning)
Distance-based Integrated Gradients -> spatially smooth feature attribution map for GeoAI models analogous to MGWR coefficients
Cross-context retrieval (in vivo / in vitro) -> cross-context service-area model retrieval for calibrating 2SFCA distance-decay without task-specific labels
Ontology-aware triplet sampling -> ICD/SNOMED hierarchy-based training for spatial disease-burden similarity model across heterogeneous health systems
Query coherence sub-cluster diagnostic -> data-fusion quality score for multi-source GPS/mobile-phone mobility embedding across cities and seasons

related.title: The Human Cell Atlas from a cell census to a unified foundation model
related.relation: companion perspective on the same HCA foundation model initiative; directly contextualizes SCimilarity's role in the HCA program

related.title: Partially shared multi-modal embedding learns holistic representation of cell state
related.relation: multi-modal cell embedding complements SCimilarity's single-modality RNA-seq metric with paired omics data

related.title: Predicting drug responses of unseen cell types through transfer learning with foundation models
related.relation: extends single-cell foundation model representations to transfer learning for drug response prediction across unseen cell types

related.title: Clustering single-cell RNA-seq data with a model-based deep learning approach
related.relation: early deep-learning approach to scRNA-seq representation learning that SCimilarity's metric-learning framework supersedes for similarity search

related.title: Scaling and quantization of a foundational deep learning model for network biology
related.relation: scaling behavior and quantization of biological foundation models, directly relevant to scaling SCimilarity beyond 23.4 million cells

related.title: Fast and precise single-cell data analysis using a hierarchical autoencoder
related.relation: hierarchical autoencoder for scRNA-seq — an alternative unsupervised representation approach that SCimilarity's metric learning outperforms for retrieval

figures.caption: SCimilarity architecture: cell querying workflow (query -> 23.4M reference -> scored results) and triplet loss training with Cell Ontology-aware anchor/positive/negative sampling.

The 23.4-million-cell reference corpus visualized as a bubble chart across 184 tissue ontology terms and 132 disease states, plus integration benchmarking versus Harmony, scVI, Scanorama, and scArches.
Cell type annotation accuracy: UMAP embeddings and confusion matrix for a held-out kidney dataset, and F1 score comparison across 15 held-out test studies versus CellTypist, TOSICA, and scANVI.

Fibrosis-associated macrophage (FM) cell search across 1,041 tissue samples from 143 studies — SCimilarity score distributions, tissue/disease bubble chart, and Integrated Gradients gene attribution scores.

Identification and experimental validation of FM-like cells in a 3D hydrogel PBMC culture system: SCimilarity scores across 40 in vitro samples, marker gene expression dot plots, and UMAP embeddings at day 0, day 5, and day 8.
